TENTATIVE RULING: Continued to 7/13/26 for failure to file amendment. All documents to be filed by 6/29/26.
EXAMINER: SEAN BANG

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Petition For Instructions To: 1. Terminate Irrevocable Trust, Or 2. In The Altrnative, Appoint A New Trustee
Off Calendar May-06-2026 Continued To Jun-08-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Mika Domingo appeared via zoom for Charles Alxander (petitioner).
Maximillian Ojeda appeared via zoom for Mechanic's Bank (objector).

Discussion was held regarding named trustees serving, that a new successor trustee shall be appointed, and that counsel to submit a proposed order to the agreed upon successor trustee. The matter is continued to June 8, 2026 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Michael McNaughton. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Singh. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Petition For Instructions To: 1. Terminate Irrevocable Trust, Or 2. In The Altrnative, Appoint A New Trustee
Off Calendar Apr-13-2026 Continued To May-06-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Petition For Instructions To: 1. Terminate Irrevocable Trust, Or 2. In The Altrnative, Appoint A New Trustee
Off Calendar Feb-23-2026 Continued To Apr-13-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Michelle Domingo for beneficiary Charles Alexander (Alik) Ilyin, who was also present.
Daniel Roberts for trustee Mechanics Bank.

The matter was called for hearing on the Petition for Instructions to: 1) Terminate Irrevocable Trust; or 2) in the Alternative, Appoint a New Trustee.

The Court stated that termination of the trust was not warranted and that the request to terminate would not be granted. The Court further stated that court involvement was not necessary to effectuate a change in trusteeship where successor trustees were named in the trust instrument and the current trustee was agreeable to stepping down.

A beneficiary addressed the Court regarding concerns about prior distributions, trustee fees, and management of the trust. The Court acknowledged the comments but stated that it was required to proceed in accordance with the trust documents.

Following discussion, the Court continued the matter to allow the parties time to determine whether a named successor trustee would assume the trusteeship, noting that if the issue was resolved without need for court intervention, the matter could be taken off calendar.

The Court continued the matter to April 13, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204 as a protective status date.

Judge: Michael B. McNaughton; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)
